name: 文殊菩萨
introduction: Manjushri is the embodiment of prajna (transcendent wisdom) of all the Buddhas. He is often depicted wielding a flaming sword that cuts through ignorance.
symbolism: Manjushri represents the perfection of wisdom, discernment, and enlightened intelligence. His sword symbolizes the ability to cut through delusion.
mantra: Om A Ra Pa Ca Na Dhih (嗡 阿 若 巴 佳 那 地)

name: 阿弥陀佛
introduction: Amitabha is the Buddha of Infinite Light, presiding over the Western Pure Land (Sukhavati). He is one of the Five Dhyani Buddhas.
symbolism: Amitabha represents infinite compassion and the promise of rebirth in the Pure Land for those who call upon his name with sincere devotion.
mantra: Namo Amitabha Buddha (南无阿弥陀佛)

name: 大日如来
introduction: Vairocana is the cosmic or primordial Buddha, representing the dharma body (dharmakaya) of the historical Buddha.
symbolism: Vairocana embodies the wisdom of the dharmadhatu — the wisdom of universal law. He represents the all-encompassing void and unity of all things.
mantra: Om Vairocana Hum (嗡 毗卢遮那 吽)

name: 不动明王
introduction: Acala (Fudō Myōō) is a wrathful deity who serves as a protector and remover of obstacles on the path to enlightenment.
symbolism: His fierce appearance represents unwavering determination to destroy evil and protect the dharma. The flames symbolize purification of all defilements.
mantra: Namah Samantavajranam Chanda Maharoshana (南无三满多 伐折罗 赧 含)

name: 观世音菩萨
introduction: Avalokitesvara is the Bodhisattva of Compassion, known as Guanyin in Chinese Buddhism. They embody the compassion of all Buddhas.
symbolism: Avalokitesvara represents infinite compassion and the willingness to help all sentient beings. The name means 'Lord who looks down' on the world with compassion.
mantra: Om Mani Padme Hum (嗡嘛呢叭咪吽)

name: 普贤菩萨
introduction: Samantabhadra is the Bodhisattva of Universal Virtue, representing the practice and meditation of all Buddhas.
symbolism: He symbolizes the completion of all vows and the embodiment of great conduct, often depicted riding a six-tusked white elephant.
mantra: Om Samantabhadra Hum (嗡 三曼多跋陀罗 吽)

name: 虚空藏菩萨
introduction: Akashagarbha is the Bodhisattva whose wisdom and merit are as vast and boundless as space itself.
symbolism: He represents the treasury of wisdom and blessings that is as infinite as space, offering guidance to those seeking knowledge.
mantra: Namo Akashagarbha Bodhisattva (南无虚空藏菩萨)

name: 大势至菩萨
introduction: Mahasthamaprapta represents the power of wisdom and is one of the three deities of the Western Pure Land alongside Amitabha and Avalokitesvara.
symbolism: He symbolizes the power of wisdom that illuminates all beings, guiding them towards enlightenment with unstoppable force.
mantra: Namo Mahasthamaprapta Bodhisattva (南无大势至菩萨)

name: 其他
introduction: This mandala contains the eight auspicious symbols (Ashtamangala) which represent the offerings made by celestial beings to Shakyamuni Buddha upon his enlightenment.
symbolism: The eight symbols — parasol, golden fishes, treasure vase, lotus, conch shell, endless knot, victory banner, and dharma wheel — each represent aspects of the Buddhist teachings.
mantra: Om Mani Padme Hum (嗡嘛呢叭咪吽)

name: 财宝天王
description: King of the North, Guardian of Wealth
introduction: Vaisravana (also known as Kubera or Jambhala's wrathful form) is the King of the North among the Four Heavenly Kings and the guardian of wealth and prosperity. He is revered as a powerful protector who bestows wealth and removes obstacles.
symbolism: Vaisravana symbolizes the protection of dharma and the bestowal of spiritual and material wealth. He rides a snow lion, representing fearlessness and majesty, and holds a victory banner and a mongoose that spews jewels.
mantra: Om Vaisravana Ye Soha (嗡 吠室罗摩拿耶 娑婆诃)

name: 释迦牟尼佛
introduction: Shakyamuni Buddha is the historical founder of Buddhism, born as Prince Siddhartha Gautama. After years of spiritual seeking, he attained enlightenment under the Bodhi tree and spent the rest of his life teaching the path to liberation.
symbolism: Shakyamuni represents the possibility of awakening for all sentient beings. His earth-touching gesture (bhumisparsha mudra) symbolizes his triumph over Mara and his enlightenment witnessed by the earth itself.
mantra: Namo Shakyamuni Buddha (南无本师释迦牟尼佛)

name: 绿度母
introduction: Green Tara is a female Bodhisattva of compassion and action, born from the tears of Avalokitesvara. She is the swift protectress who rescues beings from the eight great fears and guides them toward liberation.
symbolism: Green Tara's green color symbolizes enlightened activity and vitality. Her right foot extended represents her readiness to spring into action to help all beings. The utpala lotus she holds signifies purity and compassion.
mantra: Om Tare Tuttare Ture Soha (嗡 达咧 度达咧 度咧 梭哈)

name: 黄财神
introduction: Yellow Jambhala is the most popular form of the Buddhist wealth deity, an emanation of Ratnasambhava Buddha. He bestows wealth and prosperity upon devotees to support their spiritual practice and benefit sentient beings.
symbolism: His golden yellow body represents abundance and the richness of dharma. The mongoose (nakula) in his right hand spews precious jewels, symbolizing generosity. The citron fruit represents spiritual attainment.
mantra: Om Jambhala Jalendraye Soha (嗡 赞巴拉 扎连达耶 梭哈)

name: 光明佛母
introduction: Marici (also known as Marici Devi) is a celestial Bodhisattva associated with light, dawn, and the sun. She is a powerful protectress who can render devotees invisible to harm and obstacles.
symbolism: With three faces and eight arms, Marici embodies the power of light that dispels all darkness. Her golden boar chariot symbolizes the dawn breaking through night. She holds various weapons representing her power to overcome all obstacles.
mantra: Om Marici Ye Soha (嗡 摩利支耶 梭哈)

name: 准提佛母
introduction: Cundi Bodhisattva is revered as the Mother of Seventy Million Buddhas, a manifestation of Avalokitesvara's great compassion. With three faces and eighteen arms, she represents the perfection of all dharani practices.
symbolism: Her eighteen arms each hold different implements representing the complete range of Buddhist practices and powers. The three faces — white, red, and green — symbolize compassion, wisdom, and wrathful activity respectively.
mantra: Namo Saptanam Samyak Sambuddha Kotinam Om Cale Cule Cundi Soha (南无飒哆喃 三藐三菩陀 俱胝喃 嗡 折隶 主隶 准提 娑婆诃)

name: 地藏王菩萨
introduction: Ksitigarbha is the Bodhisattva of the Great Vow, renowned for his pledge not to achieve Buddhahood until all hells are emptied. He is the guardian of beings in the six realms of existence, especially those suffering in the hell realms.
symbolism: Ksitigarbha's vow 'If the hells are not empty, I shall not become a Buddha' represents the ultimate expression of bodhisattva compassion. He carries a wish-fulfilling jewel and a khakkhara staff to open the gates of hell.
mantra: Om Ha Ha Ha Vismaye Soha (嗡 哈哈哈 微三摩耶 梭哈)

name: 白度母
introduction: White Tara is the embodiment of longevity, healing, and compassion. She is a form of Tara with seven eyes — three on the face, one on each palm, and one on each sole — symbolizing her all-seeing awareness of the suffering of beings in all realms.
symbolism: White Tara's white body symbolizes purity and the wish-fulfilling wheel of longevity. Her seven eyes represent her omniscient awareness. She is often depicted in a meditation posture, holding a lotus with three blooms representing the Buddhas of past, present, and future.
mantra: Om Tare Tuttare Ture Mama Ayur Punye Jnana Pushtim Kuru Soha (嗡 达咧 度达咧 度咧 玛玛 阿优 布涅 嘉那 布真 咕噜 梭哈)

name: 黑财神
introduction: Black Jambhala (黑财神) is the most powerful and wrathful form of the wealth deities in Tibetan Buddhism. He is a manifestation of Buddha Akshobhya and is revered as a protector who bestows wealth upon the faithful while subjugating negative forces.
symbolism: Black Jambhala's dark blue-black body symbolizes the immutable nature of the dharmakaya. His wrathful expression represents the fierce compassion needed to overcome obstacles to prosperity. He typically holds a skull cup filled with jewels and a mongoose that spits out precious gems.
mantra: Om Jambhala Jalendraye Bashu Dharini Svaha (嗡 赞巴拉 扎连达耶 巴苏 达里尼 梭哈)
